string_table.name: npnxnanbnsnfns00000110001
string_table.string: To watch this video, use the preview feature in Storyline 360, upload your published output to a server, publish to Review 360, or switch to static video quality.
Streaming video is unavailable for local playback.
Use the w, a, s, and d keys to move around the 360 degree image. Press the tab key to jump to interactive markers and hotspots.
You must complete the question before submitting.
Enter your name in the field below:
You have reached the time limit set for the quiz. Press 'OK' to continue.

slidelink.displaytext: Process for Licensing ASCIS and LSCI
Process for Licensing ASCIS and LSCI
Seed Certification Under International Trade Agreements

resource_data.description: Here are some useful links and documents:

resource.title: Seeds Act and Seeds Regulations (Government of Canada)
Canadian Seed Growers’ Association (CSGA) – Certification Guidelines
Canadian Seed Institute (CSI) – Accreditation & Quality Assurance

terms.term: A private-sector organization licensed by CFIA to conduct seed crop inspections.
A review process conducted by CFIA to ensure that ASCIS and LSCI comply with inspection standards.
The classification of pedigreed seed (e.g., Breeder, Foundation, Registered, Certified) based on purity and generation.
Unwanted plants, weed seeds, or disease organisms found in a seed crop that could affect purity and quality.
The organization responsible for pedigreed seed certification in Canada.
The presence of foreign material or unintended plant varieties in a seed crop.
The minimum required distance between different seed varieties to prevent cross-pollination.
An individual trained and certified to conduct seed crop inspections under an ASCIS.
International certification programs that facilitate the trade of certified seed.
Seed that has been inspected and certified to meet quality and genetic purity standards.
Guidelines specifying acceptable levels of off-types, impurities, and contaminants in a seed crop.
A set of guidelines ensuring uniformity and accuracy in seed crop inspections.
A process where CFIA inspectors re-inspect a selected seed crop field to verify the accuracy of an ASCIS or LSCI inspection report.
Guidelines dictating how inspection findings must be documented and submitted to regulatory authorities.
